log.why: Mapped from structured Targeting Type.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Targeting Type. It maps to `targeting.type`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Targeting Type`. Purpose: targeting.type exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Targeting Type`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.type`. The value type is `enum`. This is expected on every spell/template instance. Because this field is required, every spell should carry a deliberate value rather than relying on absence.

acceptedValueNotes.self: Use when the canonical spell centers this targeting.type mechanic on the caster rather than a separate target.
acceptedValueNotes.single: Allowed value for targeting.type; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
acceptedValueNotes.multi: Allowed value for targeting.type; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
acceptedValueNotes.area: Allowed value for targeting.type; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
acceptedValueNotes.melee: Allowed value for targeting.type; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
acceptedValueNotes.ranged: Allowed value for targeting.type; use only when the canonical spell text supports this exact mechanic.
acceptedValueNotes.point: Use `point` for targeting.type only when the canonical spell prose supports that exact mechanic; otherwise use a more specific value or `not_applicable` if available.

log.why: Mapped from structured Targeting Max.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Targeting Max. It maps to `targeting.maxTargets`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Targeting Max`. Purpose: targeting.maxTargets exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Targeting Max`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. Use a finite number for fixed caps, a scalable object for level-based caps, and `unlimited` only for canonical any-number targeting. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.maxTargets`. The value type is `number-scalable-or-unlimited`. This is expected on every spell/template instance. Because this field is required, every spell should carry a deliberate value rather than relying on absence.

acceptedValueNotes.number: Use a finite number when the spell gives an exact target cap.
acceptedValueNotes.scalable: Use a scalable object when character level or slot level changes the target cap.
acceptedValueNotes.unlimited: Use `unlimited` only when the canonical spell prose allows any number of valid targets; do not use a fake high number such as 999.

fields.structuredLabel: Target Participation Requires Full Casting Range

log.why: Runtime targeting needs to know when selected targets must remain in range during a long casting before the spell can affect them.

fields.comment: Runtime targeting needs to know when selected targets must remain in range during a long casting before the spell can affect them. It maps to `targeting.targetParticipation.requiresWithinRangeForFullCasting`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Target Participation Requires Full Casting Range`. Purpose: targeting.targetParticipation.requiresWithinRangeForFullCasting exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Use `true` when targets must remain within range for the whole casting time, and use `not_applicable` only when the field is carried as an explicit sentinel for spells without that mechanic.

log.why: Short target-participation notes preserve the exact long-casting wording beside the runtime flag.

fields.comment: Short target-participation notes preserve the exact long-casting wording beside the runtime flag. It maps to `targeting.targetParticipation.notes`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Target Participation Notes`. Purpose: targeting.targetParticipation.notes exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about the participation rule while the boolean field remains narrow.
